rows.notes: Clear centred page heading '1838.' at very top, high contrast and unambiguous.
Clear centred section title 'Soluta Ordinaria' underlined; no amounts in this row.
Monetary columns well aligned. Pounds '4' and shillings '4' and pence '0' are clear. Description is faint/ornate; read as 'President Gov' but lettering is slightly ambiguous.
Amount aligns to shillings=1, pence=8 (no pounds). Description reads 'Anna Hutchings' though some letters are faint; chosen reading based on letter shapes and spacing.
Shillings '18' and pence '4' are legible in the shilling/pence columns; pounds blank. Description is very uncertain (faded/ornate) — transcribed as best visual match.
Columns align to pounds 4, shillings 12, pence 6. Description letters are cursive and faint; several characters uncertain, so reading is approximate.
Shillings 12 and pence 6 clearly sit in the money columns; no pounds shown. Description is partially faded; read as 'Ede Balliol' but uncertain on some letters.
Shillings '8' and pence '2' are clearly placed. Description legible though abbreviated ('Dr. o. Bapt.') and somewhat stylised.
Large round '10' in the pounds column is clear; shillings and pence are zero. Description reads as 'Supervision's Abt.' with reasonable clarity.
Alignment shows pounds 1, shillings 14, pence 8. Description is faint and the first letter unclear; 'Junction' is best-fit reading.
Numbers align to 2 pounds, 2 shillings, 0 pence. Description is longer Latin phrasing; letters are readable though ornate, year '1837' is clear.
Second successive line with '2 2 0' visible. Description is abbreviated and faint; read as 'Item Recd. 1838' given visible year and context.
Clear '25' in pounds column, zeros in shillings and pence. Description partly faded but year '1838' and phrase 'ad Fest Ann.' are visible; chosen phrasing is best match.
Second 25.0.0 line nearby; pounds 25 clear. Description is very uncertain (faded abbreviation), transcribed as approximate 'Peldem D? S. Mich.' based on visible letters.

rows.description: Leyland Fair on the Lodgings April 1838
rows.notes: Amount reads as '5 9 1 1/2' with the pence having a halfpenny loop symbol — interpreted as pence 1 with 0.5 fraction. Description partially faded; 'April 1838' and 'Fair' visible, wording otherwise reconstructed.
Second similar line with '5 9 1 1/2'. Uses 'Do.' abbreviation (ditto). Fractional halfpenny symbol visible again; description abbreviated and faint.
Clear '40' in pounds column. Description is partially legible with year '1838' and place-like names; transcription is best-effort from faded cursive.
Another clear '40 0 0'. Description reads as 'Return' and mentions 'S. Mich.' — letters clearer than many other descriptions.

rows.description: List & for the City Revenues D.1838
rows.notes: Monetary columns read 9 pounds, 11 shillings, 8 pence. Description is ornate and partly crossed with pen strokes; interpreted as relating to 'City Revenues' with year 1838 present.

rows.description: River Water from the City Works
rows.notes: Alignment of 7/7/6 is clear and fits column grid. Description 'River Water from the City Works' is legible though written in flowing cursive.
Pounds '48' clearly visible; shillings '1' and pence '6' clear. Description partly abbreviated ('Coll.') and year 1838 readable; reading 'Mines' is best-fit though faint.
Amount 1 pound 10 shillings is clear. Description starts with 'Do.' (ditto) and mentions 'Paddington School' and year '1839' — letters readable though compact.
Columns show 24/7/6 clearly. Description 'Gas Lights' and '1 year' with year 1838 are legible and consistent with ledger context.
Small amount of 0 pounds 2 shillings visible in the columns. Description is abbreviated 'Do. - Mich.' and fairly legible; monetary alignment is unambiguous.
Final total at lower right reads '269 10 3' and aligns exactly with the pounds/shillings/pence ruling lines. Description area is blank for this total line; numbers are clear.
